commit.message:
fix: separate probability from confidence; raise BET threshold in calculate_edge

- calculate_edge now requires edge ≥ 5% AND half-Kelly ≥ 2% to call a BET.
  Previously anything > 3% edge was marked BET regardless of stake size or data quality.
- Renamed output label P(outcome) so it is not confused with confidence.
- Verdict now shows the exact half-Kelly stake size (e.g. "BET — stake 3.1% of bankroll").
- System prompt explicitly forbids outputting raw probability as confidence (e.g. "Confidence: 28.6%").
- John must now state DATA QUALITY (Complete/Incomplete) and confidence level (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW)
  separately from the outcome probability.
- BET recommendation requires: edge ≥ 5%, half Kelly ≥ 2%, AND at least MEDIUM data quality.
  A 28.6% Away Win with no lineup data must come out MARGINAL or PASS, not BET.

Fix video progress tracker: update step status in DB during pipeline

Pipeline now writes step progress (pending/processing/completed/failed)
to the DB via PATCH /api/video/jobs at each step transition, so the

and marks clip-generation as failed when all clips fail.

commit.message:
Extract vertical-lift drum data from Canimex PDF catalogs

Replace incorrect VL multiplier data with PDF-extracted values using
formula: multiplier = HMA / TURNS_ON_SPRING. All 3 VL drums now have
3" increment data (vs old 6-12" increments with wrong multipliers).

Verified against all 3 Canimex calculation examples:
- Standard: exact match (IPPT=60.27, MIP=343.54)
- High-lift: exact match (mult=0.2930, turns=14.6)
- Vertical: 0.27% variance from PDF display rounding

Also fix commercial window insert logic in DoorConfigurator.

commit.message:
feat(ENG-E1): Planner Compliance - Enforce structuredDesc Population (#40)

* fix: move DB from Docker volume to bind mount — survives down -v

* feat(ENG-E1): add structuredDesc parameter to create_task and update_task MCP tools

Layer 1 of defense-in-depth: MCP tools now accept an optional structuredDesc
parameter inline with task creation/update. After task creation, if structuredDesc
is provided, it's persisted via setTaskStructuredDesc. Updated reminder-injector
to guide users when structuredDesc is omitted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(ENG-E1): add API warning system for missing structuredDesc

Layer 2: generateTaskWarnings() checks task structuredDesc for missing
aiInstructions and acceptanceCriteria. Warnings returned in API response
and surfaced through MCP create_task tool. Unit tests cover all cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(ENG-E1): add pre-flight auto-backfill for structuredDesc from markdown

Layer 3: parseMarkdownToStructuredDesc extracts aiInstructions,
acceptanceCriteria, filesInvolved, riskLevel, and estimatedEffort from
markdown descriptions. Pre-flight service auto-backfills tasks with null
structuredDesc before running checks. Results persisted to database.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(ENG-E1): update planner prompt and MCP tool descriptions for structuredDesc

Layer 4: Updated planner.md with structuredDesc schema reference table,
inline structuredDesc example in create_epic_complete call, and MUST rule
for populating structuredDesc on all tasks. Updated MCP tool descriptions
for create_task, update_task, and create_epic_complete to document
structuredDesc parameter and pre-flight importance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(ENG-E1): integration tests for structuredDesc flow and auto-backfill

Tests cover: task creation with/without structuredDesc, API warning
propagation, structuredDesc save failure handling, pre-flight auto-backfill
from markdown, and backfill skip when structuredDesc already present.
Fixes existing test expectations for check count (4→5) and required fields.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add strong warnings against using wrong script for first contact

Bot (Qwen) keeps using send_message.py instead of send_card.py for
initial peer connection. send_message.py only sends text to /message
endpoint which does NOT register the sender as a peer, causing the
remote side to see empty peers and no new cards.

- Add CRITICAL warning in Section 2: send_card.py for first contact
- Explain the difference: /card registers peer + exchanges profiles,
  /message only stores text
- Clarify send_message.py is ONLY for Section 5 conversations

commit.message:
fix: ElevenLabs TTS retry logic and voice identity protection

- Add 3-attempt retry with exponential backoff (1s/2s/4s) for ElevenLabs
  TTS synthesis, fixing intermittent failures from rate limits when
  multiple scenes are synthesized in parallel
- Stagger parallel scene starts by 1.5s each to avoid hammering the
  ElevenLabs API simultaneously
- If all retries fail, FAIL the scene instead of swapping in a random
  HeyGen voice — custom voice is tied to the user's identity/avatar
- Show actual ElevenLabs error in the UI so users know what happened
- Fix HeyGen status polling to read nested error.detail field for
  proper failure messages

commit.message:
fix: upgrade uncalibrated mu=1.0 to category default on profile creation

When a profile is created without treadwear data (mu_source=FORMULA_ESTIMATE,
estimated_mu=1.0), use the compound category's default mu instead of the
generic 1.0 sentinel. E.g., ENDURANCE_200TW gets 1.00, STREET gets 0.85.
This gives new profiles a reasonable grip estimate even without specific
tire treadwear data.

MID-131 Add tsvector search_vector column with GIN index and triggers (#85)

* MID-131 Add tsvector search_vector column with GIN index and triggers

- Alembic migration adds search_vector tsvector column to gigs table
- PL/pgSQL trigger function computes weighted search vector:
  A-weight: artist_name + linked artist names
  B-weight: support array + venue name
  D-weight: description
- Triggers on gigs, artist_gigs, venues, artists for cascading updates
- GIN index ix_gigs_search_vector for fast full-text queries
- Backfills all existing rows
- Adds search_vector to SQLAlchemy Gig model
